title: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
meta_description: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana provides ₹2 lakh life insurance cover for just ₹436 per year premium for ages 18-55.

hero.one_liner: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover (natural + accidental death) for an annual premium of just ₹436

what_is_it: PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) launched on 9 May 2015 is a pure term life insurance scheme that covers both natural and accidental death. For a premium of just ₹436 per year, you get ₹2 lakh life cover.

Unlike PMSBY which covers only accidents, PMJJBY pays out ₹2 lakh to the nominee on death due to any cause — illness, accident, or any reason. The premium is auto-debited from your bank account yearly. No medical examination is required to enroll.

eligibility.label: Requirement
eligibility.value: Savings bank account with auto-debit consent

how_to_apply.title: Visit bank or use net banking
how_to_apply.description: Available at all banks. Enroll through net banking, mobile app, or branch visit.

how_to_apply.title: Give consent for auto-debit
how_to_apply.description: Authorize ₹436 annual auto-debit. Fill nominee details.

how_to_apply.title: Cover starts immediately
how_to_apply.description: Life cover of ₹2 lakh active from June 1 to May 31. Renews annually unless you opt out.

important_alert.text: Combine PMJJBY (₹436) with PMSBY (₹20) to get ₹2L life + ₹2L accident = ₹4 lakh total cover for just ₹456/year.

faqs.question: Does this cover death due to illness?
faqs.answer: Yes. PMJJBY covers death due to ANY cause — illness, accident, natural causes. That's the key difference from PMSBY which only covers accidents.

faqs.question: Can I enroll after age 55?
faqs.answer: No. You must enroll before turning 55. If you enrolled at 50, cover continues till 55. After 55, the policy terminates.

faqs.question: What if I miss the premium payment?
faqs.answer: If ₹436 is not debited due to insufficient balance, the cover lapses. You can re-enroll by paying the premium and submitting a self-declaration of good health.

faqs.question: How much is the claim amount?
faqs.answer: ₹2 lakh paid to the nominee on death of the insured person, regardless of the cause of death.
